title: eruca sativa subsp. longirostris
intro: Eruca sativa subsp. longirostris, also known as long-beaked rocket or wild arugula, is a subspecies of the Eruca sativa plant, which is a member of the Brassicaceae family. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves, stems, and seeds, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and glucosinolates, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Eruca sativa subsp. longirostris can be consumed as a raw vegetable, used in herbal teas, or prepared as a supplement, and is also used in traditional herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions.
